name: Colorado Pharmacist License
description: Already licensed as a pharmacist? Here's whether your license transfers to Colorado - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Colorado Board of Pharmacy
provider.parentOrganization: Colorado Department of Regulatory Agencies (DORA), Division of Professions and Occupations

address.description: Colorado Department of Regulatory Agencies, Division of Professions and Occupations, 1560 Broadway, Suite 1350, Denver, CO 80202

requiredDocuments.name: Official Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Sent directly from your educational institution.

requiredDocuments.name: Exam Score Reports
requiredDocuments.description: Official verification of passing the NAPLEX and MPJE.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Colorado pharmacist license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The initial application and licensing fee varies by state. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

mainEntity.name: Is the MPJE required in all states?

acceptedAnswer.text: Almost all states require passing their state-specific Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) to verify knowledge of local pharmacy laws.

mainEntity.name: Does Colorado have reciprocity for pharmacists?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacist practice in Colorado. To work in Colorado, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacist license to Colorado?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Colorado while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
